channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike, engine fire
item.description: A FedEx cargo plane made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches political comeback with a run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo announced Saturday that he is running for mayor of New York City, relaunching his political career following a yearslong exile over a barrage of sexual harassment accusations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FedEx plane makes emergency landing after bird strike
item.description: “FedEx Flight 3609 from Newark to Indianapolis experienced a bird strike during takeoff," a FedEx spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Son of Buc-ee's co-founder charged with possession of child pornography: warrant
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KREX 5

item.title: Border czar makes the city of Boston his latest immigration target
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As Mayor Michelle Wu prepares to go to Congress to defend Boston's protections of immigrant communities, the city is in a war of words with the Trump administration and Republican representatives over how much police should support deportations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a long-standing legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries where there's war or political instability to enter and temporarily live in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pope has coffee, rests after setback in recovery
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had coffee and was reading newspapers Saturday after an alarming setback in his two-week recovery from double pneumonia: Doctors had to put him on noninvasive mechanical ventilation following a coughing fit in which he inhaled vomit that needed to then be extracted.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOD sends over 1,000 additional troops to help bolster southern border efforts
item.description: The Department of Defense is sending over 1,000 additional troops to the southern border to help bolster efforts to control illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: Federal employees are starting to receive a new email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man charged after allegedly faking seizure as puppies were stolen
item.description: A 37-year-old man has been formally charged for his alleged role in the theft of two bulldogs from a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, Fox 5

item.title: WhatsApp down Friday, according to user reports
item.description: (KRON) -- Instant messaging service WhatsApp appears to have gone down Friday, according to Downdetector.com. User reports of problems with the chat app appeared to spike Friday morning with a majority of users -- 54% -- reporting problems with sending messages.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: He floated the idea that Congress and President Trump should allow private citizens, or "pirates," to take on international cartels to protect national security.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A Tell City woman is facing charges in Vanderburgh County for allegedly marrying her biological father.
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9
